title: drimia maritima subsp. maura
intro: Drimia maritima subsp. maura, also known as the 'Cape sea squill' or 'Cape sea onion', is a subspecies of the Drimia maritima plant, a type of flowering bulbous plant native to the southwestern coast of South Africa. Traditionally, the plant has been used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Drimia maritima subsp. maura include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antiviral and antibacterial properties. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat a range of health issues.
